articles.excerpt: Pakistan has declined to accept the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang, following his release from prison. The UK government is now considering changes to immigration laws to facilitate his removal.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 9 July 2026 at 12:22. 20 source updates have been combined into this article.

Pakistan has declined to accept the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang, following his release from prison. The UK government is now considering changes to immigration laws to facilitate his removal.

Latest update

In a significant diplomatic development, Pakistan has formally refused to accept the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the leader of a Rochdale grooming gang, who was released from prison last week after serving 14 years for multiple sexual offences against minors. This refusal has raised serious concerns among victims and local communities regarding their safety and the government's capacity to manage serious offenders.

Shabir Ahmed, now 73, was convicted in 2012 for his involvement in a grooming gang that exploited vulnerable girls, some as young as 12. Following his release on July 2, the Home Office has faced mounting pressure to amend existing immigration laws to facilitate his deportation. Ahmed, who held dual British-Pakistani citizenship, was stripped of his British nationality upon conviction, leaving him solely with Pakistani citizenship.

The UK government is currently contemplating changes to the 1971 Immigration Act, which restricts the removal of certain Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK prior to 1973. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has indicated that she will propose amendments to this legislation, which could take up to a year to implement. Such changes are deemed essential to enable the deportation of Ahmed, who has been assessed by parole officers as posing a 'very high risk of serious harm to children'.

The situation has escalated into a diplomatic dispute, with reports suggesting that Pakistan is demanding the extradition of two political dissidents in exchange for accepting Ahmed back. This complicates an already sensitive issue, as the UK government must balance international relations with pressing domestic safety concerns.

Earlier developments

Local MPs and community leaders have expressed outrage over the situation, articulating fears for the safety of victims and the broader community. Calls for urgent legislative action have intensified, with some politicians arguing that current laws are outdated and fail to adequately address the severity of Ahmed's crimes. As the government faces increasing pressure to act decisively, parliamentary debates on the proposed changes to immigration law are set to take place, with the next session scheduled for July 13. The outcome of these discussions will be closely monitored by local residents and victims, who are encouraged to engage with community forums and local representatives to voice their concerns.

A police officer was taken to hospital following a collision involving a Greater Manchester Police van in Middleton on Friday, July 3. The incident occurred at approximately 1.10pm on Townley Street, where the police van collided with a car. Fortunately, the officer's injuries are not believed to be life-threatening or life-changing, and he is currently receiving assessment and treatment at the hospital. No other injuries were reported as a result of the crash, and no arrests have been made in connection with the incident.

This collision is part of a concerning trend, as it was one of two similar incidents involving police vehicles on the same day across Greater Manchester. Later that evening, at around 8.45pm, a British Transport Police van was involved in a separate crash with a blue Kia Picanto on Rochdale Road in Harpurhey. At the time of this incident, officers were transporting a 28-year-old man to police custody. Thankfully, no serious injuries were reported in this case either.

The incidents raise questions about the safety of police operations on the roads, particularly when transporting individuals in custody. The Greater Manchester Police have not released further details regarding the circumstances leading up to the Middleton crash, but it is likely that an internal review will be conducted as part of standard procedure following such incidents. This review may examine the protocols in place for police vehicle operations, especially in high-pressure situations.

Local residents may have heightened concerns regarding road safety and the operational protocols of police vehicles in light of these accidents. The implications for the community could be significant, particularly for families and commuters who rely on Townley Street and surrounding roads. If the review identifies areas for improvement, it may lead to changes in how police vehicles operate, potentially enhancing safety for all road users.

As investigations continue, the police force is expected to provide updates to ensure transparency and accountability. The community is encouraged to report any suspicious activity to help maintain safety in the area. Anyone with information about either incident is urged to contact Greater Manchester Police via their website or by calling 101. In emergencies, the public should always dial 999.

articles.title: Man Arrested Following Death of Woman in Milnrow Farm Fire
articles.excerpt: A man has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a woman was found dead in a fire at a farm in Milnrow. The incident occurred on July 3 at Higher Tunshill Farm.
articles.content_html:
A man has been arrested on suspicion of murder following the death of a woman in a fire at a farm in Milnrow. The incident took place on Friday, July 3, at Higher Tunshill Farm, located off Tunshill Lane.

Emergency services, including police, firefighters and paramedics, were called to the scene at around 12.30pm. Upon arrival, they discovered the body of a woman inside the stables. The victim has since been named as Laura Foulis, aged in her 30s.

Following the incident, a man in his 40s was taken into custody by Greater Manchester Police. He has since been bailed pending further investigation. A spokesperson for Rochdale Coroners' Court confirmed that a referral has been made regarding Ms Foulis' death, and an inquest will be opened at a later date.

Witnesses reported seeing large flames and thick black smoke rising from the stables, prompting a swift response from emergency services. Forensic investigators remained at the scene for several days following the fire, which has since been cordoned off as part of the ongoing investigation.

Tributes have been shared on social media, with friends and family remembering Ms Foulis as a kind and beautiful person who inspired many with her love of horses. One tribute described her as a 'kind soul' who made a significant impact on those around her.

The investigation into the circumstances surrounding the fire is ongoing, and police have maintained a scene at the farm as they gather evidence. Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Temporary fire station in Heywood approved during redevelopment
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service has secured approval for a temporary fire station in Heywood, ensuring continuous emergency services while the existing facility undergoes significant redevelopment.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — This report brings together confirmed developments from multiple sources.

Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) has received approval to establish a temporary fire station in Heywood, facilitating the redevelopment of the existing facility. The new structure will be situated behind the current station on Middleton Road, ensuring that emergency response services remain operational throughout the rebuilding process.

The existing fire station, which has served the Heywood community for nearly 60 years, is scheduled for demolition as part of this redevelopment initiative. The temporary station will consist of a two-storey modular building, equipped with a bay for one fire engine, while an additional outbuilding will house any specialist vehicles. This arrangement is intended to guarantee that emergency services continue without interruption during the construction period.

Planning documents reveal that the temporary fire station is strategically located just 400 metres from Junction 19 of the M62 motorway, positioning it well for rapid emergency response. GMFRS has stated that the new facility will be a modern, fit-for-purpose community fire station, designed to meet both current and future operational needs. It will also enhance facilities for staff training and community engagement, reflecting the service's commitment to adapting to the evolving demands of the community.

This redevelopment is part of a wider £38 million Estates Strategy by GMFRS, aimed at upgrading and modernising several fire stations across the Greater Manchester area. The strategy underscores the importance of ensuring that fire services can provide optimal support to residents, adapting to changing community needs and operational requirements. A separate planning application for the demolition of the existing station and the construction of the new community fire station is expected to be submitted shortly.

The anticipated new facility will include a training tower, vehicle parking, and a dedicated training yard, which will further enhance GMFRS's capabilities in the region. For local families, businesses, and schools, the uninterrupted provision of emergency services during the redevelopment is crucial, as it ensures that safety and response times remain unaffected. Local residents and stakeholders are encouraged to monitor the progress of these plans, as the redevelopment will play a vital role in maintaining safety and emergency response in Heywood. Updates will be disseminated through GMFRS and local news outlets as the project advances.

articles.title: Lift Out of Service at Central Park Tram Stop
articles.excerpt: The lift at Central Park tram stop towards Rochdale is currently out of service, affecting step-free access for passengers. Alternative routes are available for those travelling towards Oldham.
articles.content_html:
The lift at Central Park tram stop, which serves passengers travelling towards Rochdale via Oldham, is currently out of service. The disruption began on Thursday, 9 July 2026, at 8:05am and will remain in effect until further notice.

Passengers needing step-free access to the Oldham bound platform can use the lift from The Gateway to the Manchester bound platform. They will then need to use the track crossing to reach the Oldham bound platform. This arrangement is intended to assist those who may have mobility issues or require additional support while travelling.

Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) has acknowledged the inconvenience caused by this disruption and is working to resolve the issue as quickly as possible. Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available.

The Central Park tram stop is a key point for many commuters and residents in the area, linking them to both Rochdale and Oldham. The lift's unavailability may affect travel plans for those relying on step-free access, particularly during peak travel times.

Passengers are advised to plan their journeys accordingly and consider alternative routes if necessary. TfGM encourages users to check their website for live travel updates and further information regarding the lift's status.

Anyone with questions or concerns regarding their travel can find more information on the TfGM website. The service disruption is a reminder of the importance of accessible transport options for all users.

articles.title: Heywood couple uses football to strengthen bonds with foster children
articles.excerpt: Lauren and Danny Sullivan have harnessed their passion for football to create a nurturing environment for young people in their care, fostering connections and easing transitions into family life since 2012.
articles.content_html:
Lauren and Danny Sullivan, a fostering couple from Heywood, have underscored the significant role that football plays in forging strong connections with the young people they care for. Since embarking on their fostering journey with Rochdale Borough Council in 2012, the couple has welcomed five boys, aged between 12 and 21, into their home, alongside their nine-year-old son, Daniel. Football has become a cornerstone of their daily routine, with the family actively participating in coaching, training sessions, and weekend matches.

The Sullivans have expressed their delight in witnessing the joy on the children's faces as they engage in play together in their garden after school or college. They have observed that football not only nurtures relationships but also aids children in adjusting to their new surroundings. A particularly memorable event was a Champions League final party held to welcome their first long-term foster child, which played a crucial role in easing his transition into the family.

Danny, who works as a football coach and scout, has taken an active interest in mentoring one of the 17-year-olds in their care, assisting him in obtaining coaching qualifications and becoming a grassroots coach. This mentorship has fostered a strong bond between them, facilitating discussions about coaching techniques and personal development. Another young person, who initially communicated solely in French, was able to integrate into the family swiftly through their shared love of football. Lauren noted that this common activity effectively broke down language barriers, enabling him to connect with the other boys almost immediately.

The couple hopes to inspire others to consider fostering, highlighting the transformative impact that love and support can have on young people within the care system. Their commitment to nurturing these relationships through football not only enriches the lives of the children but also contributes positively to the wider community.

Councillor Rachel Massey from Rochdale Borough Council commended the Sullivans for their dedication, emphasising the vital difference that foster carers make in providing stability and a sense of belonging for vulnerable children. As the need for foster families continues to grow, initiatives like those undertaken by the Sullivans may encourage more individuals to consider this rewarding path, potentially leading to improved outcomes for children in care across the borough.

Manager Jim Bentley welcomed Maguire to the club, noting his impressive qualities and the positive impact he can have on the squad. Bentley emphasised the importance of strengthening the defence as the team aims for a successful campaign this season.

articles.title: Bluetick Coonhounds and Chugs available for adoption in Castleton
articles.excerpt: Two breeds of dogs, Bluetick Coonhounds and Chugs, are currently available for adoption in Castleton, Vermont, as listed on Petfinder.
articles.content_html:
Petfinder has announced the availability of two dog breeds for adoption in Castleton, Vermont: Bluetick Coonhounds and Chugs. Both breeds are currently seeking loving homes, offering potential adopters a chance to provide a new life for these animals in need.

Bluetick Coonhounds are distinguished by their striking blue-spotted coats and renowned for their exceptional tracking abilities. These dogs are known for their friendly and energetic disposition, making them well-suited for active households. Prospective adopters should consider the exercise requirements and social nature of Bluetick Coonhounds, as they thrive in environments where they receive ample physical activity and companionship.

Chugs, a hybrid breed resulting from the cross between Chihuahuas and Pugs, present a different set of characteristics. Smaller in stature, Chugs are celebrated for their affectionate and playful personalities. They adapt well to various living situations, including apartments, making them ideal companions for both families and individuals.

The adoption process typically begins with an application submitted through Petfinder, where interested individuals can gain insights into each dog's specific needs and personality traits. Meeting the dogs in person is highly recommended to ensure a suitable match between the pet and the adopter. For those in Castleton considering adoption, the Petfinder website offers comprehensive details regarding the adoption process, including any associated fees and requirements, as well as guidance on preparing for a new pet.

Adopting a pet not only provides a loving home for an animal but also plays a crucial role in alleviating the issue of homeless pets within the community. As the demand for pet adoption rises, local shelters and rescue organisations are increasingly in need of volunteers and support. Community members who wish to contribute can contact local animal shelters or visit their websites for further information on how to assist in these efforts. This engagement not only benefits the animals but also fosters a sense of community responsibility and compassion.

Established over 60 years ago, Bleakholt Animal Sanctuary has been dedicated to providing care, shelter, and rehoming services for hundreds of animals. In addition to its core mission, the sanctuary engages in educational and community outreach, further solidifying its role within the local area. Karen Weed, the Sanctuary Manager, conveyed her gratitude for the continued partnership, emphasising the significant impact that the funds raised have on the lives of the animals in their care.

The renewed partnership will feature a variety of fundraising events and awareness campaigns designed to support the sanctuary's mission and highlight its contributions to the local community. The first year of collaboration included numerous events that not only generated financial support but also heightened public awareness regarding animal welfare and the services provided by Bleakholt.

This partnership is likely to enhance community spirit and encourage local residents to support a charity that plays a vital role in animal welfare. The commitment from The Rock Shopping Centre underscores the importance of local businesses in championing charitable causes and fostering community engagement. Residents interested in supporting Bleakholt Animal Sanctuary can participate in upcoming events at The Rock Shopping Centre, which will be announced throughout the year. articles.title: Proposal for new SEND school at Kingsway West Business Park
articles.excerpt: Whitehall Academy has submitted plans to convert a vacant warehouse into a school for children with special educational needs and disabilities, accommodating up to 50 pupils.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — This report brings together confirmed developments from multiple sources.

A proposal has been submitted to transform a vacant warehouse at Kingsway West Business Park in Rochdale into a new educational facility for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND). The plans, put forward by Whitehall Academy, aim to create a school that will accommodate up to 50 pupils, providing essential support for children with complex needs.

The design of the proposed school includes six classrooms, three sensory rooms, and a multi-purpose hall, alongside various support spaces such as offices and a medical room. While the exterior of the building will remain unchanged, a secure play area featuring artificial grass is planned for the west side, utilising previously unused space. This initiative represents an extension of Whitehall Academy's existing operations on Whitehall Street, where the independent primary school already focuses on supporting vulnerable children with diverse needs.

Students at the proposed facility may include those diagnosed with autism and learning difficulties, all of whom will have an Education, Health, and Care Plan (EHCP) or be undergoing the assessment process. The school is expected to operate from 9am to 3pm for pupils, with staff hours extending from 8am to 4pm. There are also plans for breakfast and after-school clubs, which could extend the school day until 5pm, providing additional support for families.

The warehouse, previously occupied by Circuitmaster Designs Ltd, an engineering firm, has been vacant since October 2024. The conversion of this space into a SEND school is likely to address a pressing need for specialised educational facilities in the area, particularly as the demand for such services continues to grow.

The establishment of a SEND school at Kingsway West Business Park could have significant implications for local families and the wider community. Parents of children with special educational needs may find increased access to tailored educational resources and support, reducing the need for travel to distant facilities. Additionally, the school could foster a more inclusive environment within the neighbourhood, promoting awareness and understanding of SEND issues. As the proposal progresses, local stakeholders, including parents and community organisations, may wish to engage with the planning process to ensure that the needs of all affected parties are considered.

A small handheld fan overheated at Wardle Academy on Thursday, leading to the temporary evacuation of students from the school. The incident prompted the school to seek advice from Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service, which attended the scene to assess the situation.

In a message sent to parents and carers, the school explained that the evacuation was a precautionary measure while the overheating fan was assessed. Firefighters confirmed that the building was safe for students and staff to return shortly after their arrival.

Initial reports on social media suggested that the fan had exploded; however, the school clarified that the incident involved an overheating device rather than an explosion. No injuries were reported as a result of the incident.

Teaching resumed soon after the fire service confirmed the safety of the building. The school expressed satisfaction with how students responded during the evacuation, stating that all learners remained calm throughout the process.

Wardle Academy has reassured parents and guardians that safety is a priority and that appropriate measures were taken to ensure the well-being of students during the incident. The school is committed to maintaining a safe learning environment for all pupils.

The council has committed to closely monitoring the trial, with plans to gather feedback from residents to evaluate the scheme's impact. A review is scheduled to take place at the end of the trial period, which will determine whether the changes will be made permanent or adjusted based on community input. Local residents can submit their views through the council's official channels, ensuring that all perspectives are considered in this important safety initiative.

The implications of this trial scheme extend beyond mere traffic management. Families, particularly those with children, may find the changes reassuring if they lead to improved safety. However, businesses in the area could experience fluctuations in customer traffic if congestion increases, potentially affecting their operations. Schools nearby may also need to adapt to any changes in travel patterns, as parents and guardians navigate the new traffic flow. Overall, the outcome of this trial could significantly influence daily life in the Hopwood Triangle, making community feedback a crucial element in shaping the future of this initiative.

articles.excerpt: The Kirkholt Pantry has celebrated its 10th anniversary, recognising a decade of essential food assistance to local families amid ongoing economic challenges.
articles.content_html:
The Kirkholt Pantry recently commemorated its 10th anniversary with a special event at The Strand, celebrating a decade of unwavering support for families in the Kirkholt area. This community initiative has been instrumental in providing essential food assistance, particularly during a period of rising living costs that has placed significant financial strain on many households.

Since its establishment, the pantry has distributed over 23,000 bags of essential food items, offering affordable access to groceries for those in need. Each week, members can collect a bag of vital food supplies for just £4, making it an indispensable resource for numerous families facing economic hardship. The anniversary event brought together staff, patrons, and volunteers, including some of the original founders of the pantry, highlighting the collaborative spirit that has sustained this initiative.

Councillor Daniel Meredith, a key supporter of the pantry through donations and funding from Rochdale South Township, expressed his pride in the initiative. He referred to the pantry as a "lifeline for local residents," underscoring its critical role in alleviating financial pressures on families. In a message shared on social media, Councillor Meredith extended his gratitude to the volunteers and donors who have contributed to the pantry's success over the years, describing the project as a "shining example of community spirit and partnership in action."

The Kirkholt Pantry operates every Thursday from 10am to 12pm, continuing to serve as a vital resource for families grappling with economic difficulties. As the cost of living continues to escalate, the pantry's role in helping households manage their budgets and access essential food items is likely to become even more significant. Families relying on this service may find it increasingly important as they navigate the challenges posed by rising prices.

Looking to the future, the Kirkholt Pantry aims to expand its services to reach even more families in need. Community members are encouraged to support the pantry through donations or by volunteering, ensuring that this essential service can continue to thrive and adapt to the evolving needs of the local population. The ongoing commitment to supporting families in Kirkholt reflects a broader community effort to address food insecurity and foster resilience in the face of economic challenges.

articles.title: Calls for Change to Rochdale Rape Law from Former Attorneys General
articles.excerpt: Former attorneys general have called for changes to the law surrounding rape cases in Rochdale, highlighting concerns over current legal frameworks and their effectiveness in securing justice.
articles.content_html:
Former attorneys general have called for changes to the law regarding rape cases in Rochdale. They argue that the current legal framework is inadequate and does not effectively serve victims of sexual violence.

The comments come in light of ongoing discussions about the handling of rape cases within the legal system. The former officials expressed concern that existing laws may not provide sufficient support for victims seeking justice.

One former attorney general stated that the law must evolve to better reflect the complexities of modern cases. They emphasised the need for a system that prioritises the needs and rights of victims, ensuring they feel safe and supported throughout the legal process.

In recent years, Rochdale has faced scrutiny over its handling of sexual offences, particularly in relation to high-profile cases. The former attorneys general believe that reform is necessary to restore public confidence in the justice system.

The proposed changes could involve revising legal definitions, improving victim support services, and enhancing training for law enforcement and legal professionals. These adjustments aim to create a more responsive and effective legal environment for those affected by sexual violence.

As discussions continue, the implications of these proposed changes could have a significant impact on how rape cases are prosecuted in Rochdale and beyond. Stakeholders are encouraged to engage in the conversation to ensure that the voices of victims are heard.

articles.title: Taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport: a comprehensive guide
articles.excerpt: A detailed overview of taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport, covering routes, fares, and booking options for local travellers.
articles.content_html:
Residents of Rochdale planning to travel to Manchester Airport can now refer to a detailed guide outlining the available taxi services for this route. The guide encompasses various routes, estimated fares, and booking options, enabling travellers to make informed decisions regarding their transportation.

The most frequently used taxi routes from Rochdale to Manchester Airport typically take between 30 to 45 minutes, contingent on traffic conditions. The A627(M) and M62 are the primary roads utilised, offering direct access to the airport. Travellers should consider checking real-time traffic updates to mitigate potential delays, particularly during peak travel periods.

Fares for taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport can fluctuate considerably based on the time of day and the specific taxi company selected. On average, passengers can anticipate costs ranging from £30 to £50 for a standard taxi. It is prudent to compare prices from various providers and consider pre-booking to secure the most favourable rates.

In addition to traditional taxi services, ride-sharing options are available for those seeking a more flexible approach. These services can be booked through mobile applications, allowing users to track their ride and manage payment options directly via their smartphones. This flexibility may appeal to families and individuals with varying schedules, enhancing convenience for all travellers.

As travel patterns continue to evolve, it is vital for Rochdale residents to remain aware of the best transportation options at their disposal. Reliable taxi services can significantly improve the travel experience, whether for business or leisure. For further information or to book a taxi, residents can visit local taxi company websites or contact them directly for assistance. This accessibility ensures that families, commuters, and businesses can plan their journeys effectively, minimising stress and maximising efficiency.

articles.title: Trial begins for man accused of murdering teenager in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King is on trial for the alleged murder of 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who was struck by King's car after stealing an e-bike.
articles.content_html:
A trial has commenced at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court for Terrence King, who faces allegations of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines in Middleton last year. The incident occurred on 22 July 2025, when Simmonds-Caines was struck by King's vehicle after allegedly stealing an e-bike from another boy.

The prosecution, led by Michael Hayton KC, contends that King, 60, deliberately drove his car at Simmonds-Caines as an act of revenge. CCTV and doorbell camera footage presented in court reportedly shows King accelerating before veering onto the pavement and colliding with the teenager, who succumbed to his injuries at the scene.

King has admitted to manslaughter, acknowledging that his actions resulted in Simmonds-Caines's death, but he denies the charge of murder. The prosecution argues that this incident was not merely a tragic accident or a case of poor driving, but rather a calculated act of aggression against a boy who had stolen the e-bike.

The footage shown in court was described as distressing, prompting some family members of Simmonds-Caines to leave the courtroom in tears. This case has attracted significant attention, particularly due to the victim's age and the circumstances surrounding his death, raising broader concerns about youth crime and vigilantism.

The community in Middleton is now grappling with the implications of such incidents, which raise pressing questions about safety and the appropriate responses to crime. The trial is expected to continue over the coming weeks, with further evidence and testimonies to be presented. Local residents, particularly those affected by the incident, may be closely following the proceedings as they seek to comprehend the full context of this tragic event and its impact on community safety.

articles.title: Wrestlers Attend Summer Clinic in Castleton, Vermont
articles.excerpt: Ten local wrestlers are participating in a summer clinic in Castleton, Vermont, where they had the opportunity to learn from NCAA All-American Tyler Knox.
articles.content_html:
Ten wrestlers from the local area are currently attending a summer clinic in Castleton, Vermont. The clinic provides young athletes with the chance to enhance their skills and learn from experienced professionals in the sport.

Among the highlights of the clinic is a session with Tyler Knox, a notable wrestler from Stanford University. Knox, who is an NCAA All-American, placed eighth in the 133-pound category as a redshirt freshman in 2025.

The clinic aims to develop the participants' wrestling techniques and strategies, offering them valuable insights from a successful athlete like Knox. This opportunity is particularly significant for the young wrestlers, as they can gain knowledge that may benefit their future competitions.

Local coaches have expressed enthusiasm about the clinic, noting that experiences like this can inspire and motivate young athletes. The chance to learn from accomplished wrestlers is seen as a vital part of their development.

As the clinic progresses, the wrestlers will engage in various training sessions designed to improve their performance on the mat. This experience is expected to contribute positively to their skills and confidence.

The summer clinic not only serves as a training ground but also fosters camaraderie among the participants, allowing them to bond over their shared passion for wrestling.
